implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0
requirements_addressed: AC-001 four functions in operations.py returning GitOpResult
AC-002 every git call goes through the injected execute callable; no subprocess.run / os.system / shell=True
AC-003 cwd is the build worktree (or the source repo for prepare_worktree)
AC-004 prepare_worktree runs git worktree add and returns worktree_path
AC-005 commit_all runs add/commit/rev-parse and returns sha
AC-006 push runs git push origin <branch> and converts non-zero into status=failed with stderr
AC-007 cleanup_worktree runs git worktree remove --force best-effort: logs warning, returns failed, never raises
AC-008 every function is wrapped in try/except Exception with type:msg in stderr (ADR-ARCH-025)
AC-009 shell metacharacters pass as literal argv tokens (verified by unit test)
AC-010 unit tests with fake execute cover success, non-zero exit, raised exception, and shell-metacharacter passthrough for each operation
AC-011 black + ruff + mypy all report clean against both files
Seam tests (@pytest.mark.seam @pytest.mark.integration_contract('git_adapter_subprocess_contract')) hit a real git binary

completion_promises.criterion_text: All four functions in src/forge/adapters/git/operations.py, returning GitOpResult
completion_promises.evidence: src/forge/adapters/git/operations.py defines prepare_worktree, commit_all, push, and cleanup_worktree. Each is async and the return type annotation is GitOpResult (re-exported from forge.adapters.git.models). Verified by tests TestPrepareWorktree::test_success_populates_worktree_path, TestCommitAll::test_success_returns_sha_from_rev_parse, TestPush::test_success_returns_success_with_zero_exit, TestCleanupWorktree::test_success_removes_worktree.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Every call goes through DeepAgents execute (no subprocess.run / os.system direct usage — permissions are enforced by the framework)
completion_promises.evidence: All four operations accept a keyword-only `execute: ExecuteCallable` and delegate every git invocation through it. The default `_default_execute` uses asyncio.create_subprocess_exec (no shell, list tokens) so the in-module surface honours the same contract. TestAdapterContract::test_no_subprocess_run_or_os_system_in_module source-scans operations.py and rejects 'subprocess.run(', 'os.system(', and 'shell=True,' / 'shell=True)'. The test fixtures inject a FakeExecute and the assertion side checks that the recorded calls are the only path used.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All calls run with cwd inside the build's worktree (Scenario 'Subprocesses are executed inside the current build's worktree')
completion_promises.evidence: commit_all, push, and cleanup_worktree pass cwd=str(worktree) (cleanup uses worktree.parent so the to-be-removed path is not the cwd). prepare_worktree is the documented exception: the worktree does not yet exist, so cwd=str(repo) (source repo). TestCommitAll::test_success_returns_sha_from_rev_parse asserts every recorded call's cwd matches str(worktree); TestPush::test_success_returns_success_with_zero_exit asserts the same for push; TestPrepareWorktree::test_command_is_list_tokens_and_cwd_is_repo asserts cwd=str(repo) for prepare_worktree.

completion_promises.criterion_text: prepare_worktree creates /var/forge/builds/{build_id}/, runs git worktree add <path> <branch>, returns GitOpResult.worktree_path
completion_promises.evidence: prepare_worktree builds the path as `builds_root / build_id` (default builds_root = Path('/var/forge/builds')), issues `['git', 'worktree', 'add', str(path), branch]` via the injected execute, and on exit_code 0 returns GitOpResult(status='success', operation='prepare_worktree', worktree_path=str(path), exit_code=0). TestPrepareWorktree::test_success_populates_worktree_path and test_command_is_list_tokens_and_cwd_is_repo verify this. Builds_root is overridable for tests but defaults to /var/forge/builds in production.

completion_promises.criterion_text: commit_all runs git add -A && git commit -m <msg> and returns GitOpResult.sha (parsed from git rev-parse HEAD after the commit)
completion_promises.evidence: commit_all issues three sequential execute calls: ['git','add','-A'], ['git','commit','-m', message], ['git','rev-parse','HEAD']. The third call's stdout is stripped and assigned to GitOpResult.sha on success. TestCommitAll::test_success_returns_sha_from_rev_parse verifies the call sequence and that sha == 'abc1234567890abcdef'. The 'nothing to commit' real-world case is exercised by TestCommitAll::test_commit_with_nothing_to_commit_returns_failed_with_stdout (preserves stdout in stderr field of GitOpResult).

completion_promises.criterion_text: push runs git push origin <branch> and converts non-zero exit into status='failed' with stderr preserved
completion_promises.evidence: push issues ['git','push','origin', remote_branch] and on non-zero exit returns GitOpResult(status='failed', operation='push', stderr=<stderr-or-stdout>, exit_code=<code>). TestPush::test_non_zero_exit_preserves_stderr feeds exit_code=1 + stderr='! [rejected] non-fast-forward' and asserts status=='failed', exit_code==1, 'rejected' in result.stderr.

completion_promises.criterion_text: cleanup_worktree runs git worktree remove <path> --force and returns status='failed' on non-zero exit but logs and continues
completion_promises.evidence: cleanup_worktree issues ['git','worktree','remove', str(worktree), '--force'] from worktree.parent. On non-zero exit it logger.warnings with prefix 'cleanup_worktree non-zero exit (best-effort, ...)' and returns GitOpResult(status='failed', ...) without raising. On exception it logger.exceptions 'cleanup_worktree exception (best-effort, ...)' and returns GitOpResult(status='failed', stderr='<Type>: <msg>'). TestCleanupWorktree::test_failure_logs_warning_and_returns_failed_without_raising and test_exception_in_execute_logs_and_returns_failed verify both branches; the seam test test_seam_cleanup_worktree_failure_does_not_block exercises the contract against a real git binary on a non-existent path.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Each function is wrapped in try/except Exception as exc returning GitOpResult(status='failed', operation=..., stderr=f'{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}') — never raises (ADR-ARCH-025)
completion_promises.evidence: All four operations have a try: ... except Exception as exc: ... block ending in return _exception_failure(operation, exc), which constructs GitOpResult(status='failed', operation=<op>, stderr=f'{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}', exit_code=-1). TestPrepareWorktree::test_exception_in_execute_does_not_raise (OSError -> 'OSError: disk gone'), TestCommitAll::test_exception_in_execute_does_not_raise (RuntimeError -> 'RuntimeError: kaboom'), TestPush::test_exception_in_execute_does_not_raise (ConnectionError -> 'ConnectionError: network down'), and TestCleanupWorktree::test_exception_in_execute_logs_and_returns_failed (OSError -> 'OSError: permission denied') verify each.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Arguments containing shell metacharacters are passed as separate list tokens to execute, never via shell-string concatenation
completion_promises.evidence: Every operation builds `command=['git', ...]` with each argument as a discrete list element — there is no f-string concatenation into a shell line and the default executor uses create_subprocess_exec (no shell). TestCommitAll::test_message_with_shell_metacharacters_passed_as_literal_token feeds nasty='feat: pwn; rm -rf / && echo "owned"' and asserts (a) commit_call.command == ['git','commit','-m', nasty] and (b) commit_call.command[3] is nasty (identity check — the message survives untouched as a single argv slot). TestAdapterContract::test_no_subprocess_run_or_os_system_in_module rejects 'shell=True,' / 'shell=True)' in the source.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Unit tests with a fake execute: success path for each op, non-zero exit, raised exception, shell-metacharacter argument passthrough
completion_promises.evidence: tests/forge/adapters/git/test_operations.py defines a FakeExecute dataclass that records every call and replays scripted ExecuteResults / raised exceptions. Coverage matrix: prepare_worktree (success / non-zero / exception), commit_all (success / add-fail / commit-fail-with-stdout / exception / shell-metacharacter), push (success / non-zero / exception), cleanup_worktree (success / non-zero-best-effort / exception). 16 unit tests + 2 seam tests = 18 total, all passing.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: Both files are formatted with Black (the project-canonical formatter per .claude/rules/code-style.md: 'Use Black formatter, Max line length: 88 characters'). Verified with `python3 -m black --target-version py312 --check src/forge/adapters/git/operations.py tests/forge/adapters/git/test_operations.py` -> 'All done! ✨ 🍰 ✨ 2 files would be left unchanged'. Additionally `python3 -m ruff check ...` reports 'All checks passed!' and `python3 -m mypy --ignore-missing-imports src/forge/adapters/git/operations.py` reports 'Success: no issues found in 1 source file'. After formatting, the full 1032-test repo suite passes with no regressions and the 18 module tests still pass.
